1029.reason: [Missing right parenthesis error is recorded, but the parser does not consume or skip the incorrect token, which may cause subsequent parsing to fail or produce incorrect results. Additionally, the line number should correspond to the 'main' token (children.get(1)) rather than the 'int' token (children.get(0)).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The error line number should correspond to the line of 'main' or '(', not the 'int' token at children.get(0). Also, the parser doesn't consume any token after detecting the missing ')', which may cause further parsing issues.]

1030.reason: [When there's a missing right parenthesis error, the parser doesn't consume any token but should continue parsing. However, more critically, if RPARENT is found, the code consumes it with lexer.move(1), but if not found, it doesn't advance, which may cause infinite loops or incorrect parsing. Additionally, after reporting error j, it should ideally simulate recovery by assuming the RPARENT exists and proceed.]

866.reason: [Incorrect lexer positioning: should only move back by 1 since only one token was consumed with getAndNext(), moving back by 2 will point to wrong position and cause incorrect error line reporting or parsing issues]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect lexer positioning: should only move back by 1 since only one token was consumed with getAndNext(), moving back by 2 will point to wrong position and cause incorrect error line reporting or parsing issues]

867.reason: [The error line number should correspond to the last non-terminal in the expression (which is 'root'), not the current lexer position after moving back. Using lexer.get().getLine() here may point to an incorrect line, especially when the expression spans multiple lines. It should use root.getLine() instead to correctly identify the line of the last non-terminal before the missing semicolon.]
